titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Czechoslovakian Press Clippings Collection
titlestmt.author: compiled by William E. Brown, Jr.

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 09:06

did.abstract: The collection consists of mimeographed copies of press reports from Czechoslovakian sources. The material was collected by David Korbel, an attorney who served as Secretary General of the Czechoslovak State Council (in exile) after the German occupation.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Czechoslovakian Press Clippings Collection (MS 1420). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the Slavic and East European Collection, Sterling Memorial Library, 1985.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of mimeographed copies of press clippings and reports from Czechoslovakian sources. These materials document cultural, social, and political topics relating to Czechoslovakia from 1954 through 1968. There are no English translations for any of the items.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: The collection was compiled by David Korbel, an attorney who served as Secretary General of the Czechoslovak State Council (in exile) after the German occupation. The materials were transferred to the Manuscripts and Archives Department from the Slavic and East European Collection in 1985.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.
